title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: But the {{c1::economic impact seems}}(经济影响似乎) to be {{c2::dramatically behind}}(远远落后). It’s very difficult to {{c3::make sense of}}(理解), how can the model, on the one hand, do these amazing things, and then on the other hand, repeat itself twice in some situation?
original: But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind. It’s very difficult to make sense of, how can the model, on the one hand, do these amazing things, and then on the other hand, repeat itself twice in some situation?
translation: 但经济影响似乎远远落后。很难理解，模型怎么可能在一方面做出这么惊人的成果，另一方面却在某些情况下重复自己两次？
note:
phrases: economic impact seems | dramatically behind | make sense of
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source: 
chunk: chunk_0004_b5466e
source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: abstraction_bridge | stance_positioning
transfer: Useful for lifting specific examples into a broader claim.
expression_transfer: Useful for lifting specific examples into a broader claim.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: You look at the evals and you go, “Those are {{c1::pretty hard evals}}(相当难的评估).” They are {{c2::doing so well}}(做得这么好). But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind.
original: You look at the evals and you go, “Those are pretty hard evals.” They are doing so well. But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind.
translation: 你来看看评测结果，就会想：“这些评测挺难的啊。”它们表现得这么好。但经济影响似乎远远落后。
note:
phrases: pretty hard evals | doing so well
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever We get used to things pretty fast, {{c1::it turns out}}(结果发现). But also it’s kind of abstract. What does it mean?
original: Ilya Sutskever We get used to things pretty fast, it turns out. But also it’s kind of abstract. What does it mean?
translation: 伊利亚·苏茨克弗 我们发现自己很快就能适应新事物。但这也有点抽象，这意味着什么？
note:
phrases: it turns out

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever {{c1::Don’t you think}}(你不觉得) so? All this AI stuff and all this Bay Area… that it’s happening.
original: Ilya Sutskever Don’t you think so? All this AI stuff and all this Bay Area… that it’s happening.
translation: 伊利亚·苏茨克弗 你难道不这么认为吗？所有这些AI技术和硅谷的一切……这些事情正在发生。

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Dwarkesh Patel {{c1::Should we actually}}(我们是否应该) begin here? I think this is an interesting discussion.
original: Dwarkesh Patel Should we actually begin here? I think this is an interesting discussion.
translation: Dwarkesh Patel 我们应该从这里开始吗？我认为这是一个有趣的讨论。

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: I think the {{c1::impact of AI}}(人工智能的影响) is going to be felt. AI is going to be {{c2::diffused through the economy}}(在经济中扩散).
original: I think the impact of AI is going to be felt. AI is going to be diffused through the economy.
translation: 我认为AI的影响将会被感受到。AI将会在整个经济中扩散。
note:
phrases: impact of AI | diffused through the economy

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: An example would be, {{c1::let’s say}}(假设) you {{c2::use vibe coding}}(使用振动编码) to do something. You go to some place and then you {{c3::get a bug}}(发现一个 bug).
original: An example would be, let’s say you use vibe coding to do something. You go to some place and then you get a bug.
translation: 例如，假设你使用vibe编码来做某些事情。你去某个地方，然后遇到了一个bug。

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: But there is another explanation. {{c1::Back when people}}(在人们) were doing pre-training, the question of what data to train on was answered, because that answer was everything.
original: But there is another explanation. Back when people were doing pre-training, the question of what data to train on was answered, because that answer was everything.
translation: 但还有另一种解释。在人们进行预训练的时候，训练数据的问题已经解决了，因为那个答案是所有数据。
note:
phrases: Back when people

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever — {{c1::We're moving}}(我们正在) from the age of scaling to the age of research – Explaining model jaggedness Ilya Sutskever You know what’s crazy? Dwarkesh Patel Meaning what?
original: Ilya Sutskever — We're moving from the age of scaling to the age of research – Explaining model jaggedness Ilya Sutskever You know what’s crazy? Dwarkesh Patel Meaning what?
translation:
伊利亚·苏茨克弗（Ilya Sutskever）——我们正从规模化时代进入研究时代——解释模型锯齿状现象
伊利亚·苏茨克弗
你知道什么最疯狂吗？德瓦尔凯什·帕特尔
什么意思？
note:
phrases: We're moving
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: From what I hear, all the companies have teams that just produce {{c1::new RL environments}}(新的强化学习环境) and just add it to the training mix. The question is, well, what are those? There are so many degrees of freedom.
original: From what I hear, all the companies have teams that just produce new RL environments and just add it to the training mix. The question is, well, what are those? There are so many degrees of freedom.
translation: 据我了解，所有公司都有团队专门开发新的强化学习环境，并将其加入训练混合中。问题是，这些环境到底是什么？自由度实在太多了。
note:
phrases: new RL environments
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source: 
chunk: chunk_0009_caedb9
source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: strong_collocation
transfer: Useful for producing native-like collocations in formal discussion.
expression_transfer: Useful for producing native-like collocations in formal discussion.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: There’ll be very strong {{c1::economic forces for}}(经济力量) this, and I think the impact is going to be felt very strongly. Dwarkesh Patel When do you expect that impact?
original: There’ll be very strong economic forces for this, and I think the impact is going to be felt very strongly. Dwarkesh Patel When do you expect that impact?
translation:
这将产生非常强大的经济力量，我认为影响将会非常显著。德瓦尔凯什·帕特尔
你预计什么时候会出现这种影响？
note:
phrases: economic forces for

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: This is one of the very confusing things about the models right now. How to reconcile the fact that they are {{c1::doing so well}}(做得这么好) on evals?
original: This is one of the very confusing things about the models right now. How to reconcile the fact that they are doing so well on evals?
translation: 这正是当前模型中非常令人困惑的一点：如何解释它们在评估中表现如此出色的事实？
note:
phrases: doing so well
title: Ilya-Sutskever

target_lang: zh

expression_transfer: Useful for producing native-like collocations in formal discussion.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: I have {{c1::two possible explanations}}(两种可能的解释). The {{c2::more whimsical explanation}}(更奇特的解释) is that maybe RL training makes the models a little too single-minded and narrowly focused, a little bit too unaware, even though it also makes them aware in some other ways.
original: I have two possible explanations. The more whimsical explanation is that maybe RL training makes the models a little too single-minded and narrowly focused, a little bit too unaware, even though it also makes them aware in some other ways.
translation: 我有两种可能的解释。更有趣的解释是，也许强化学习训练让模型变得过于专注，过于狭隘，在某种程度上甚至让它们变得不那么敏感，尽管它也让它们在其他方面变得更加敏感。
note:
phrases: two possible explanations | more whimsical explanation
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever No, {{c1::I don’t think}}(我认为) so. Dwarkesh Patel Okay, interesting.
original: Ilya Sutskever No, I don’t think so. Dwarkesh Patel Okay, interesting.
translation: 伊利亚·苏茨克维尔：不，我不这么认为。德瓦尔凯什·帕特尔：好的，有趣。

phrases: I don’t think

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Back when people were {{c1::doing pre-training}}(进行预训练), the question of what data to {{c2::train on was answered}}(训练在回答), because that answer was everything. When you do pre-training, you {{c3::need all the data}}(需要所有的数据).
original: Back when people were doing pre-training, the question of what data to train on was answered, because that answer was everything. When you do pre-training, you need all the data.
translation: 在人们进行预训练的时候，训练数据的问题已经解决了，因为答案是所有数据。当你进行预训练时，你需要所有的数据。
note:
phrases: doing pre-training | train on was answered | need all the data

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Dwarkesh Patel Another thing that’s crazy is how normal the slow takeoff feels. The idea that we’d be investing 1% of GDP in AI, {{c1::I feel like}}(我觉得) it would have felt like a bigger deal, whereas right now it just feels...
original: Dwarkesh Patel Another thing that’s crazy is how normal the slow takeoff feels. The idea that we’d be investing 1% of GDP in AI, I feel like it would have felt like a bigger deal, whereas right now it just feels...
translation: 德瓦尔凯什·帕特尔：另一件疯狂的事情是，缓慢的爆发增长看起来是如此的正常。这个想法，即我们将在人工智能上投资占GDP的1%，我感觉这本应该是一件大事，然而现在却只是感觉...
note:
phrases: I feel like
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever The thing which I was referring to not feeling different is, okay, such and such company announced some difficult-to-comprehend {{c1::dollar amount of investment}}(投资金额). I don’t think anyone knows what to do with that.
original: Ilya Sutskever The thing which I was referring to not feeling different is, okay, such and such company announced some difficult-to-comprehend dollar amount of investment. I don’t think anyone knows what to do with that.
translation: 伊利亚·苏茨克维尔 我提到的那种不觉得有什么不同的情况是，比如某某公司宣布了一大笔难以理解的投资金额。我觉得没人知道该如何利用这些资金。
note:
phrases: dollar amount of investment

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: But when people do {{c1::RL training}}(强化学习训练), they do need to think. They say, “Okay, we want to have {{c2::this kind of RL training}}(这种强化学习训练) for this thing and that kind of RL training for that thing.”
original: But when people do RL training, they do need to think. They say, “Okay, we want to have this kind of RL training for this thing and that kind of RL training for that thing.”
translation: 但当人们进行强化学习训练时，确实需要动脑筋。他们会说：“好的，我们想要这种类型的强化学习训练用于这个任务，那种类型的强化学习训练用于那个任务。”
note:
phrases: RL training | this kind of RL training
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source:

source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: strong_collocation
transfer: Useful for producing native-like collocations in formal discussion.
expression_transfer: Useful for producing native-like collocations in formal discussion.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: How to {{c1::reconcile the fact}}(调和这一事实) that they are doing so well on evals? You look at the evals and you go, “Those are pretty hard evals.”
original: How to reconcile the fact that they are doing so well on evals? You look at the evals and you go, “Those are pretty hard evals.”
translation: 如何调和他们在评估测试中表现这么好的事实呢？你看看这些评估测试，就会想：“这些评估测试看起来挺难的呀。”
note:
phrases: reconcile the fact

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Then you tell the model, “Can you please {{c1::fix the bug}}(修复这个错误)?” And the model says, “Oh my God, you’re so right. I have a bug. Let me {{c2::go fix that}}(去修复那个).”
original: Then you tell the model, “Can you please fix the bug?” And the model says, “Oh my God, you’re so right. I have a bug. Let me go fix that.”
translation: 然后你告诉模型：“能不能请你修复这个bug？”模型就会说：“哦我的天，您说得对。我确实有个bug。让我去修复它。”
note:
phrases: fix the bug | go fix that
